findings.title: Siloed-borrowing risk controls are defined but never enforced on borrow

best_agent_match.title: Siloed-borrowing risk controls are dead code and can be bypassed entirely

findings.title: ERC721 liquidation can seize NFT collateral using any listed ERC20 without repaying or even bounding against actual debt

best_agent_match.title: ERC721 liquidation lets the caller choose any reserve asset as payment instead of repaying an actual debt asset

findings.title: supplyERC721FromNToken is bricked by `ownerOf` on nonexistent nToken ids

best_agent_match.title: supplyERC721FromNToken is bricked because validation calls ownerOf() on an unminted NToken id

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect debt calculation uses balanceOf instead of scaledBalanceOf
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported. `IVariableDebtToken` explicitly exposes `balanceOf(user)` as the current debt view, and this helper is used where current debt is needed. The separate `scaledBalanceOf` path in `GenericLogic` is an optimization, not evidence that `balanceOf` is wrong here.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer overflow in GenericLogic liquidity threshold calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable. The cited multiplications are executed under Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic, so overflow reverts instead of silently corrupting health factor calculations.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero when liquidation bonus is zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Only arises from administrator misconfiguration of reserve parameters. The code does not show an attacker-triggerable path, so this is not a protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in FlashClaimLogic
rejected_candidates.reason: Insufficiently supported. The report does not identify a concrete state-corruption path, and the repository snapshot does not expose a vulnerable public `flashClaim` entrypoint that would make the callback reentrancy claim reportable on its own.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy in marketplace flash loan pattern
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported. The public marketplace entrypoints in `PoolMarketplace` are protected by `nonReentrant`, so a generic callback/reentrancy claim against `_buyWithCredit` is not enough.

rejected_candidates.title: NFT transferred before state update in supply
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable. If `executeSupplyERC721Base` reverts, the prior `safeTransferFrom` is reverted as part of the same transaction, so the NFT is not permanently stranded by this ordering alone.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked return value in MarketplaceLogic
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. `Address.functionDelegateCall` reverts on failure; the delegatecall result is not silently ignored.

rejected_candidates.title: Repay with pTokens allows repaying more than debt
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. `paybackAmount` is initialized to actual debt and only reduced when `params.amount` is smaller, so repayment is capped at `min(requestedAmount, variableDebt)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing initialization check in ParaProxy constructor
rejected_candidates.reason: Deployment self-misconfiguration only. Passing `address(0)` as owner does not create an attacker-triggerable protocol issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential division by zero in GenericLogic
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported. The cited divisions are guarded by explicit nonzero checks on the relevant collateral totals, and the ERC721 health-factor denominator is separately guarded by `payableDebtByERC20Assets >= totalDebtInBaseCurrency`.
